body:
### Problem

Neovim version 0.12.3

When ever I do yank in neovim while `vim._core.ui2` is enabled I always see my status line being disappeared until and unless I move my cursor. This problem also occurs when a plugin uses the cmdline to message such as `[osc52] 935 characters copied`. 

### Steps to reproduce

Initialization : 
```sh
mkdir ~/.config/tmp  && cd ~/.config/tmp/
nvim init.lua 
```

Configuration : 
```lua
require('vim._core.ui2').enable()
vim.o.cmdheight = 0
```

Save and exit and launch with : 

```sh
NVIM_APPNAME=tmp nvim README.md
``` 

paste some paragraphs : 

```txt
The old clock on the wall ticked slower than usual. Rain tapped against the window like impatient fingers.

Somewhere in the distance, a train whistled—low and mournful.

She turned the page, but the words had already faded into a dream.
````

Do the following motions : 

```
ggVGy
```

output in cmdline will blank out the statusline : 

```

19 lines yanked
```

### Expected behavior

The expected behaviour is to still display the statusline despite any message.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

nvim -v NVIM v0.12.3 Build type: Release LuaJIT 2.1.1780076327 Run "nvim -V1 -v" for more info

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

I am unsure about whether Vim behaves like that or not.

### Operating system/version

Android aarch64

### Terminal name/version

Termux 0.118.3 

### $TERM environment variable

echo $TERM                                          xterm-256color

### Installation

apt install neovim

labels.description: Nvim 0.12 mesages/dialog/pager/notifications based on the UI-ext protocol
labels.name: ui2

title: Status line disappearing on yanking while `vim._core.ui2` is enabled on neovim 0.12.3.
